question: Which historical period is identified as the time when Avestan texts were finally written down after oral transmission?

choices.A: They are unrelated but share vocabulary
choices.B: Old Avestan is derived from Vedic Sanskrit
choices.C: They are closely related sister languages with similar grammar and lexicon
choices.D: Vedic Sanskrit is a dialect of Avestan

question: What misunderstanding led to the older use of the term “Zend” for Avestan?

choices.B: Mistaking commentary texts for the language itself

question: Why is the classification of Avestan as “Eastern Iranian” considered limited by some scholars?

choices.C: Later east–west distinctions had not yet developed

question: Which Mongolian root is proposed as a possible origin of the word “baklava”?

choices.B: Uses milk-soaked layers instead of baked pastry layers
